verses.latin_vulgate: Veniat dilectus meus in hortum suum,  et comedat fructum pomorum suorum.     Veni in hortum meum, soror mea, sponsa ;  messui myrrham meam cum aromatibus meis ;  comedi favum cum melle meo ;  bibi vinum meum cum lacte meo ;  comedite, amici, et bibite,  et inebriamini, carissimi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בָּ֣אתִי לְגַנִּי֮ אֲחֹתִ֣י כַלָּה֒ אָרִ֤יתִי מוֹרִי֙ עִם־בְּשָׂמִ֔י אָכַ֤לְתִּי יַעְרִי֙ עִם־דִּבְשִׁ֔י שָׁתִ֥יתִי יֵינִ֖י עִם־חֲלָבִ֑י אִכְל֣וּ רֵעִ֔ים שְׁת֥וּ וְשִׁכְר֖וּ דּוֹדִֽים׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Let my beloved come into his garden, and eat the fruit of his apple trees. I am come into my garden, O my sister, my spouse, I have gathered my myrrh, with my aromatical spices: I have eaten the honeycomb with my honey, I have drunk my wine with my milk: eat, O friends, and drink, and be inebriated, my dearly beloved.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä tulin, sisareni, rakas morsiameni, yrttitarhaani, ja olen mirrhamini ja yrttini leikannut ylös. Minä olen syönyt mesileipäni hunajan kanssa: minä olen juonut viinani minun maitoni kanssa: syökäät armaani, ja juokaat ystäväni, ja juopukaat.
verses.king_james: I am come into my garden, my sister, my spouse: I have gathered my myrrh with my spice; I have eaten my honeycomb with my honey; I have drunk my wine with my milk: eat, O friends; drink, yea, drink abundantly, O beloved.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego dormio, et cor meum vigilat.  Vox dilecti mei pulsantis :     Aperi mihi, soror mea, amica mea,  columba mea, immaculata mea,  quia caput meum plenum est rore,  et cincinni mei guttis noctium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲנִ֥י יְשֵׁנָ֖ה וְלִבִּ֣י עֵ֑ר ק֣וֹל ׀ דּוֹדִ֣י דוֹפֵ֗ק פִּתְחִי־לִ֞י אֲחֹתִ֤י רַעְיָתִי֙ יוֹנָתִ֣י תַמָּתִ֔י שֶׁרֹּאשִׁי֙ נִמְלָא־טָ֔ל קְוֻצּוֹתַ֖י רְסִ֥יסֵי לָֽיְלָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: I sleep, and my heart watcheth: the voice of my beloved knocking: Open to me, my sister, my love, my dove, my undefiled: for my head is full of dew, and my locks of the drops of the nights.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä makaan, ja minun sydämeni valvoo; se on ystäväni ääni, joka kolkuttaa. Avaa minun eteeni, sisareni, armaani, kyhkyläiseni, ihanaiseni; sillä minun pääni on täynnä kastetta, ja minun palmikkoni täynnä yön pisaria.
verses.king_james: I sleep, but my heart waketh: it is the voice of my beloved that knocketh, saying, Open to me, my sister, my love, my dove, my undefiled: for my head is filled with dew, and my locks with the drops of the night.

verses.latin_vulgate: Expoliavi me tunica mea : quomodo induar illa ?  lavi pedes meos : quomodo inquinabo illos ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פָּשַׁ֙טְתִּי֙ אֶת־כֻּתׇּנְתִּ֔י אֵיכָ֖כָה אֶלְבָּשֶׁ֑נָּה רָחַ֥צְתִּי אֶת־רַגְלַ֖י אֵיכָ֥כָה אֲטַנְּפֵֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: I have put off my garment, how shall I put it on? I have washed my feet, how shall I defile them?
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä olen riisunut hameeni, kuinka minun pitäis taas pukeman ylleni? Minä olen viruttanut jalkani, kuinka minun pitäis sokaiseman ne jälleen?
verses.king_james: I have put off my coat; how shall I put it on? I have washed my feet; how shall I defile them?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dilectus meus misit manum suam per foramen,  et venter meus intremuit ad tactum ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דּוֹדִ֗י שָׁלַ֤ח יָדוֹ֙ מִן־הַחֹ֔ר וּמֵעַ֖י הָמ֥וּ עָלָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: My beloved put his hand through the key hole, and my bowels were moved at his touch.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta minun ystäväni pisti kätensä lävestä, ja minun sisällykseni vapisivat siitä.
verses.king_james: My beloved put in his hand by the hole of the door, and my bowels were moved for him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Surrexi ut aperirem dilecto meo ;  manus meæ stillaverunt myrrham,  et digiti mei pleni myrrha probatissima.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קַ֥מְתִּֽי אֲנִ֖י לִפְתֹּ֣חַ לְדוֹדִ֑י וְיָדַ֣י נָֽטְפוּ־מ֗וֹר וְאֶצְבְּעֹתַי֙ מ֣וֹר עֹבֵ֔ר עַ֖ל כַּפּ֥וֹת הַמַּנְעֽוּל׃
verses.douay_rheims: I arose up to open to my beloved: my hands dropped with myrrh, and my fingers were full of the choicest myrrh.
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin nousin minä avaamaan ystävälleni. Minun käteni tiukkuivat mirrhamia, ja mirrham valui minun sormistani lukon salvan päälle.
verses.king_james: I rose up to open to my beloved; and my hands dropped with myrrh, and my fingers with sweet smelling myrrh, upon the handles of the lock.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pessulum ostii mei aperui dilecto meo,  at ille declinaverat, atque transierat.  Anima mea liquefacta est, ut locutus est ;  quæsivi, et non inveni illum ;  vocavi, et non respondit mihi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פָּתַ֤חְתִּֽי אֲנִי֙ לְדוֹדִ֔י וְדוֹדִ֖י חָמַ֣ק עָבָ֑ר נַפְשִׁי֙ יָֽצְאָ֣ה בְדַבְּר֔וֹ בִּקַּשְׁתִּ֙יהוּ֙ וְלֹ֣א מְצָאתִ֔יהוּ קְרָאתִ֖יו וְלֹ֥א עָנָֽנִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: I opened the bolt of my door to my beloved: but he had turned aside, and was gone. My soul melted when he spoke: I sought him, and found him not: I called, and he did not answer me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin minä ystävälleni avasin, oli ystäväni mennyt pois ja vaeltanut ohitse; niin läksi sieluni ulos hänen sanansa jälkeen; minä etsin häntä, mutta en löytänyt häntä: minä huusin, mutta ei hän minua vastannut.
verses.king_james: I opened to my beloved; but my beloved had withdrawn himself, and was gone: my soul failed when he spake: I sought him, but I could not find him; I called him, but he gave me no answer.

verses.latin_vulgate: Invenerunt me custodes qui circumeunt civitatem ;  percusserunt me, et vulneraverunt me.  Tulerunt pallium meum mihi custodes murorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מְצָאֻ֧נִי הַשֹּׁמְרִ֛ים הַסֹּבְבִ֥ים בָּעִ֖יר הִכּ֣וּנִי פְצָע֑וּנִי נָשְׂא֤וּ אֶת־רְדִידִי֙ מֵֽעָלַ֔י שֹׁמְרֵ֖י הַחֹמֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: The keepers that go about the city found me: they struck me: and wounded me: the keepers of the walls took away my veil from me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vartiat, jotka käyvät kaupunkia ympäri, löysivät minun, löivät minua ja haavoittivat minun; ja vartiat muurin päällä ottivat pois minun pääliinani.
verses.king_james: The watchmen that went about the city found me, they smote me, they wounded me; the keepers of the walls took away my veil from me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Adjuro vos, filiæ Jerusalem,  si inveneritis dilectum meum,  ut nuntietis ei quia amore langueo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִשְׁבַּ֥עְתִּי אֶתְכֶ֖ם בְּנ֣וֹת יְרוּשָׁלָ֑͏ִם אִֽם־תִּמְצְאוּ֙ אֶת־דּוֹדִ֔י מַה־תַּגִּ֣ידוּ ל֔וֹ שֶׁחוֹלַ֥ת אַהֲבָ֖ה אָֽנִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: I adjure you, O daughters of Jerusalem, if you find my beloved, that you tell him that I languish with love.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä vannotan teitä, Jerusalemin tyttäret, jos te löydätte minun ystäväni, niin ilmoittakaat hänelle, että minä olen sairas rakkaudesta.
verses.king_james: I charge you, O daughters of Jerusalem, if ye find my beloved, that ye tell him, that I am sick of love.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qualis est dilectus tuus ex dilecto, o pulcherrima mulierum ?  qualis est dilectus tuus ex dilecto, quia sic adjurasti nos ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַה־דּוֹדֵ֣ךְ מִדּ֔וֹד הַיָּפָ֖ה בַּנָּשִׁ֑ים מַה־דּוֹדֵ֣ךְ מִדּ֔וֹד שֶׁכָּ֖כָה הִשְׁבַּעְתָּֽנוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: What manner of one is thy beloved of the beloved, O thou most beautiful among women? what manner of one is thy beloved of the beloved, that thou hast so adjured us?
verses.finnish_biblia: Millinen on sinun ystäväs muiden ystävien suhteen, sinä kaikkein ihanaisin vaimoin seassa? Minkäkaltainen on sinun ystäväs muiden ystävien suhteen, ettäs meitä olet niin vannottanut?
verses.king_james: What is thy beloved more than another beloved, O thou fairest among women? what is thy beloved more than another beloved, that thou dost so charge us?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dilectus meus candidus et rubicundus ;  electus ex millibus.
verses.douay_rheims: My beloved is white and ruddy, chosen out of thousands.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun ystäväni on valkia ja punainen, kaunistettu kymmenentuhannen seassa.
verses.king_james: My beloved is white and ruddy, the chiefest among ten thousand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Caput ejus aurum optimum ;  comæ ejus sicut elatæ palmarum, nigræ quasi corvus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רֹאשׁ֖וֹ כֶּ֣תֶם פָּ֑ז קְוֻצּוֹתָיו֙ תַּלְתַּלִּ֔ים שְׁחֹר֖וֹת כָּעוֹרֵֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: His head is as the finest gold: his locks as branches of palm trees, black as a raven.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hänen päänsä on paras ja kallein kulta; hänen hiuksensa ovat kaharat ja mustat niinkuin kaarne.
verses.king_james: His head is as the most fine gold, his locks are bushy, and black as a raven.

verses.latin_vulgate: Oculi ejus sicut columbæ super rivulos aquarum,  quæ lacte sunt lotæ, et resident juxta fluenta plenissima.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֵינָ֕יו כְּיוֹנִ֖ים עַל־אֲפִ֣יקֵי מָ֑יִם רֹֽחֲצוֹת֙ בֶּֽחָלָ֔ב יֹשְׁב֖וֹת עַל־מִלֵּֽאת׃
verses.douay_rheims: His eyes as doves upon brooks of waters, which are washed with milk, and sit beside the plentiful streams.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hänen silmänsä ovat niinkuin mettisen silmät, vesiojan tykönä, rieskalla pestyt, ja ovat täynnä.
verses.king_james: His eyes are as the eyes of doves by the rivers of waters, washed with milk, and fitly set.

verses.latin_vulgate: Genæ illius sicut areolæ aromatum,  consitæ a pigmentariis.  Labia ejus lilia,  distillantia myrrham primam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְחָיָו֙ כַּעֲרוּגַ֣ת הַבֹּ֔שֶׂם מִגְדְּל֖וֹת מֶרְקָחִ֑ים שִׂפְתוֹתָיו֙ שֽׁוֹשַׁנִּ֔ים נֹטְפ֖וֹת מ֥וֹר עֹבֵֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: His cheeks are as beds of aromatical spices set by the perfumers. His lips are as lilies dropping choice myrrh.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hänen poskensa ovat niinkuin kaunistettu ryytimaa, niinkuin yrttein kukkaset; hänen huulensa ovat niinkuin ruusut, jotka vuotavat mirrhamin pisaroita.
verses.king_james: His cheeks are as a bed of spices, as sweet flowers: his lips like lilies, dropping sweet smelling myrrh.

verses.latin_vulgate: Manus illius tornatiles, aureæ,  plenæ hyacinthis.  Venter ejus eburneus,  distinctus sapphiris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יָדָיו֙ גְּלִילֵ֣י זָהָ֔ב מְמֻלָּאִ֖ים בַּתַּרְשִׁ֑ישׁ מֵעָיו֙ עֶ֣שֶׁת שֵׁ֔ן מְעֻלֶּ֖פֶת סַפִּירִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: His hands are turned and as of gold, full of hyacinths. His belly as of ivory, set with sapphires.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hänen kätensä ovat niinkuin kultaiset sormukset, täynnä turkosia; hänen ihonsa on niinkuin puhdas elephantin luu, saphirilla kaunistettu.
verses.king_james: His hands are as gold rings set with the beryl: his belly is as bright ivory overlaid with sapphires.

verses.latin_vulgate: Crura illius columnæ marmoreæ  quæ fundatæ sunt super bases aureas.  Species ejus ut Libani,  electus ut cedri.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שׁוֹקָיו֙ עַמּ֣וּדֵי שֵׁ֔שׁ מְיֻסָּדִ֖ים עַל־אַדְנֵי־פָ֑ז מַרְאֵ֙הוּ֙ כַּלְּבָנ֔וֹן בָּח֖וּר כָּאֲרָזִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: His legs as pillars of marble, that are set upon bases of gold. His form as of Libanus, excellent as the cedars.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hänen jalkansa ovat niinkuin marmoripatsaat, perustetut kultaisten jalkain päälle; hänen hahmonsa on niinkuin Libanon ja valittu sedri.
verses.king_james: His legs are as pillars of marble, set upon sockets of fine gold: his countenance is as Lebanon, excellent as the cedars.

verses.latin_vulgate: Guttur illius suavissimum,  et totus desiderabilis.  Talis est dilectus meus,  et ipse est amicus meus, filiæ Jerusalem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חִכּוֹ֙ מַֽמְתַקִּ֔ים וְכֻלּ֖וֹ מַחֲמַדִּ֑ים זֶ֤ה דוֹדִי֙ וְזֶ֣ה רֵעִ֔י בְּנ֖וֹת יְרוּשָׁלָֽ͏ִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: His throat most sweet, and he is all lovely: such is my beloved, and he is my friend, O ye daughters of Jerusalem.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hänen suunsa on suloinen, ja hän itse kokonansa ihanainen. Senkaltainen on minun ystäväni, minun rakkaani on senkaltainen, te Jerusalemin tyttäret.
verses.king_james: His mouth is most sweet: yea, he is altogether lovely. This is my beloved, and this is my friend, O daughters of Jerusalem.
